output: Implemented batch 1 tasks T1 and T3. Singular shot creation now uses the atomic `create_shot_with_generations` flow with direct cache upserts, and the new `duplicate_as_new_generation` SQL migration is added with in-function collision resolution and hydrated return data. Verification is checkpointed; the shot-creation test module still has two expected failures in pending T2 test files, and `npx supabase db push --linked` did not complete in this environment.
deviations: Did not update failing shot-creation tests because that work is explicitly assigned to T2 in a later batch.

Attempted `npx supabase db push --linked`, but it produced no output and did not complete under the current environment, so the new SQL migration was not verified against the linked database in this batch.

task_updates.executor_notes: Singular shot creation now delegates to the `create_shot_with_generations` RPC path with `generationIds:[generationId]`, so the cache flow is shared with the plural path: RPC result -> `mapRpcShotGenerationToRow`/`buildShotStats` -> `upsertShotInCache` -> `enqueueGenerationsInvalidation(includeShots:false, includeProjectUnified:true)` -> `invalidateShotsQueries(refetchType:'inactive')`. Removed the singular action wiring from the old `createShotWithImage` mutation and switched the composite hook to use `createShotWithGenerations` for both singular and plural generation creation. `npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/` fails only in the two tests still asserting the retired pre-T2 singular-path API.

Added `duplicate_as_new_generation` as a new migration at `supabase/migrations/20260406_duplicate_as_new_generation.sql`. The RPC validates project ownership, loads source variant data with generation fallback, builds params with `source:'timeline_duplicate'`, creates the duplicated generation and primary variant in one function, serializes per-shot frame assignment with an advisory transaction lock, preserves the midpoint/explicit-target/+30 target-frame logic, probes higher then lower frames while `offset < 1000`, and returns the new generation/shot_generation ids plus location/thumbnail/type/params/created_at for later cache hydration. Attempted `npx supabase db push --linked`, but it did not complete in this environment.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `createShotWithGenerationPath` now funnels directly into `createShotWithGenerationsPath`, so the singular flow uses the exact shared five-step sequence already implemented there: RPC call, shot-generation mapping, `upsertShotInCache`, `enqueueGenerationsInvalidation(... includeShots:false ...)`, then `invalidateShotsQueries(... refetchType:'inactive' ...)`.

Yes. The SQL RPC computes the target frame with explicit-target or midpoint-or-+30 logic, serializes per-shot collision resolution with `pg_advisory_xact_lock`, searches higher then lower frames while `offset < 1000`, and returns `new_generation_id`, `new_shot_generation_id`, `timeline_frame`, `location`, `thumbnail_url`, `type`, `params`, and `created_at`.
